ground_truth.title: Headphone - Sweep, Loft, and Extrude
ground_truth.main_content: This tutorial demonstrates how to create surfaces from profile curves using Rhino. You will learn how to use sub-object selection to preselect objects for a command, create a surface from a planar curve, loft, revolve, sweep, and extrude surfaces, cap planar holes to create a solid, draw a helix around a curve, match curve ends, create solid pipes, mirror objects, use layers, and use object snaps. The tutorial covers creating the speaker shell using lofted surface, one-rail sweep, solid extrusion of a planar curve, and surface fillet. It then demonstrates creating the padding and cover by sweeping a curve around the speaker cone edge. The mounting bracket is created through extrusion, filleting, and pipe commands. The headband is formed by arraying ellipses along a path and sweeping them, then rounding the ends using revolve and mirror. Finally, the speaker wire is created using helix, match, and pipe commands, and all parts are mirrored to complete the headphones.
with: This tutorial demonstrates how to create surfaces from profile curves.
One way to create a surface is to use existing curves as a guide.
When lofting through curves, the curves are used as a guide for creating a smooth surface.
Surfaces that share an edge can be joined into a polysurface.
The headband consists of a series of ellipses swept along a path.
without: Rhino for Windows © 2010-2018 Robert McNeel & Associates
